title: Apple sues OpenAI for trade secret theft and illegal employee recruitment
summary: The lawsuit accuses OpenAI of orchestrating a campaign for former Apple employees to take confidential hardware information.

title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT Work and shuts down Atlas browser to simplify its product offering
summary: The company unifies its artificial intelligence tools but faces criticism for the confusion generated among ChatGPT, Atlas, and Codex.

title: SK Hynix raises $26.5 billion in the largest foreign IPO in the US and predicts memory shortage until 2030
summary: The South Korean HBM chipmaker debuted on Nasdaq and its CEO warned that the semiconductor shortage will worsen before improving.

title: Meta removes Instagram feature that generated AI images from public photos
summary: The tool, which used user posts to train AI models without explicit consent, was removed after controversy.

title: OpenAI introduces GPT-5.6 with Sol, Terra, and Luna models, and Sol proves a mathematical conjecture
summary: The new AI system introduces five levels of reasoning and enables a smaller model to be autonomously trained by the main model.

title: OpenAI's head of security leaves the company
summary: The departure of the executive occurs in a context of restructuring and increased attention to safety in the development of artificial intelligence.

title: Steam sales reach $11 billion in the first half of 2026
summary: Growth driven by Chinese players and the retro catalog solidifies Valve's platform against its competitors.

title: Microsoft emissions increase 25% due to boom in data centers for AI
summary: The increase jeopardizes the 2030 sustainability goal, although the company assures it remains feasible with new strategies.

title: New York passes 'Click to Cancel' law to prohibit deceptive subscriptions and hidden charges
summary: The regulation requires companies to offer cancellation as easily as sign-up, in a milestone for consumer protection.

title: Tencent negotiates purchase of Manus from Meta after Chinese government intervention
summary: The Chinese company aims to acquire a majority stake in the artificial intelligence startup after Beijing forced Meta to divest its investment.

title: EU demands Meta redesign Facebook and Instagram, threatens fines for addictive design
summary: European authorities consider that the platforms' algorithms exploit user vulnerability and order substantial changes.

title: China advances in reusable rockets and recovers Long March 10B booster with a net at sea
summary: The success of the marine recovery positions China as a direct competitor to SpaceX in launch vehicle reusability.

title: Flock surveillance cameras mistakenly identify a car critic and police detain him
summary: An error in license plate recognition caused the journalist to be surrounded by police in a parking lot and held for an hour.

title: FCC authorizes test of giant mirror satellite to reflect sunlight back to Earth
summary: The experiment, approved despite complaints from astronomers, aims to illuminate dark areas at night using an orbital reflector.

title: Meta launches Muse Spark 1.1, an open-source model that outperforms GLM-5.2 in programming
summary: The model offers 1 million tokens of context and improves orchestration of autonomous tasks with reduced computational cost.

title: Users abandon smartphones for basic phones and landlines in search of digital disconnection
summary: The 'dumb phone' movement gains followers as a response to exhaustion from social media and constant overstimulation.

title: The government department DOGE officially shuts down operations
summary: The entity created to modernize public administration ceases its activities, according to official sources.

title: European Parliament approves Chat Control 1.0 without public debate, critics say
summary: The communications surveillance regulation was adopted through an accelerated procedure, generating rejection among privacy advocates.

title: OpenAI could run out of cash by mid-2027, according to financial analysis
summary: High infrastructure costs and lack of sustainable revenues jeopardize the company's viability in the short term.

title: SpaceX requests to launch 100,000 more Starlink satellites to multiply bandwidth by 100
summary: The proposal for massive expansion of the constellation raises concerns about light pollution and orbital safety.
